sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.122 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: For nilpotent subgroups$A,B, C$of a finite group$G$, let$_G(A,B,C)$be the subgroup of$A$generated by all minimal by inclusion intersections of the form$A B^x C^y$, where$x, y G$, and let$_G(A,B,C)$be the subgroup of$_G(A,B,C)$generated by all intersections of this kind of minimal order. [r]{a)} Is it true that$_G(A,B,C) F(G)$? [r]{b)} Is it true that$_G(A,B,C) F(G)$? [r]{c)} The same questions for...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
